name: vscode-operator
description: AI that operates VS Code like a real developer through Language Model Tools and a local MCP bridge.

commands.title: VSCode Operator: Show MCP Bridge Status

vscodeOperator.mcpBridge.enabled.description: Enable the local MCP bridge that exposes VS Code language model tools to external MCP clients.

vscodeOperator.mcpBridge.host.description: Host interface for the local MCP bridge. Keep this on 127.0.0.1 unless you intentionally want remote access.

vscodeOperator.mcpBridge.port.description: TCP port for the local MCP bridge. Set to 0 to let the OS choose a free port.

vscodeOperator.mcpBridge.path.description: HTTP path for the MCP endpoint exposed by the local bridge.

languageModelTools.name: vscodeOperator_readProblems
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call this tool BEFORE attempting any fix, refactor, or code-quality task. It returns all diagnostics from the VS Code Problems panel (errors, warnings, hints) with file path, severity, message, source, code, and precise line/column range. Always read problems first when the user reports build errors, type errors, lint issues, or asks you to fix the codebase.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read the current VS Code Problems panel diagnostics.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of diagnostics to return

languageModelTools.name: vscodeOperator_runSupportedCommand
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call this tool to get rich editor context with human-readable output. Use action=activeEditorSummary to get the currently open file, language, and cursor. Use action=hoverTopVisible for hover info at the top visible line. Use action=hoverAtPosition with line+column (1-based) for hover info at a specific position. Use action=completionAt with line+column (and optional triggerCharacter) to get LSP completions at a position — call this ONCE per unfamiliar type at the start of a coding task to discover correct member names, rather than before every individual method call. Always follow tools/list inputSchema exactly; unsupported fields are rejected. Prefer this tool over vscodeOperator_executeCommand for these actions.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run optimized VS Code command actions.

action.description: Supported action. Use completionAt to query LSP member completions before generating method/property accesses.

column.description: 1-based column number (required for hoverAtPosition and completionAt)

triggerCharacter.description: Optional trigger character for completionAt (e.g. ":" for Lua method call, "." for property access)

languageModelTools.name: vscodeOperator_executeCommand
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call this tool to execute any VS Code command by its command id when no specialized tool covers the need. Examples: open a file (vscode.open), show a definition (vscode.executeDefinitionProvider), trigger a code action (editor.action.quickFix), or inspect symbol references. Pass arguments as the args array or as argsJson (JSON-encoded array string). The tool returns the command result as text. Use vscodeOperator_runSupportedCommand first for hoverTopVisible and activeEditorSummary.

argsJson.description: Alternative JSON string for args, must decode to an array
